definition: Equity valuation regimes are structurally driven by the discount rate and inflation regime. Three post-war regimes: 1950-68 (P/E 16-18x, low inflation), 1969-82 (P/E 8-12x, high inflation), 1983-2021 (P/E 18-24x, low inflation). Current ~21-22x forward P/E implicitly prices a negative stock-bond correlation environment.
evidence: Three post-war regime transitions each lasting 2-4 years with >30% drawdowns
Rolling stock-bond correlation oscillating between -0.2 and +0.4 post-2022
Fair value at ~18-19x if correlation regime shifted persistently positive, implying 10-25% overvaluation
open_questions: Whether correlation regime has durably shifted positive
Whether AI productivity gains could sustain higher growth expectations offsetting higher discount rates
Whether 2-3 more years of inflation data needed to confirm Regime D
